author: śrīla śrīnivāsācārya
verses:
kṛṣṇotkīrtana⋅gāna⋅nartana⋅parau premāmṛtāmbhonidhī
dhīrādhīra⋅jana⋅priyau priya⋅karau nirmatsarau pūjitau
śrī⋅caitanya⋅kṛpā⋅bharau bhuvi bhuvobhārāvahantārakau
vande rūpa⋅sanātanau raghu⋅yugau śrī⋅jīva⋅gopālakau

nānāśāstra⋅vicāraṇaika⋅nipuṇau saddharma⋅saṁsthāpakau
lokānāṁ hita⋅kāriṇau tribhuvane mānyau śaraṇyākarau
rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa⋅padāravinda⋅bhajanānandena mattālikau
vande rūpa⋅sanātanau raghu⋅yugau śrī⋅jīva⋅gopālakau

śrī⋅gaurāṅga⋅guṇānuvarṇana⋅vidhau śraddhā⋅samṛddhyanvitau
pāpottāpa⋅nikṛntanau tanubhṛtāṁ govinda⋅gānāmṛtaiḥ
ānandāmbudhi⋅vardhanaika⋅nipuṇau kaivalya⋅nistārakau
vande rūpa⋅sanātanau raghu⋅yugau śrī⋅jīva⋅gopālakau

tyaktvā tūrṇamaśeṣa⋅maṇḍala⋅pati⋅śreṇīṁ sadā tucchavat
bhūtvā dīna⋅gaṇeśakau karuṇayā kaupīna⋅kanthāśritau
gopībhāva⋅rasāmṛtābdhi⋅laharī⋅kallola⋅magnau muhur⋅
vande rūpa⋅sanātanau raghu⋅yugau śrī⋅jīva⋅gopālakau

kūjat⋅kokila⋅haṁsa⋅sārasa⋅gaṇākīrṇe mayūrākule
nānāratna⋅nibaddha⋅mūla⋅viṭapa⋅śrīyukta⋅vṛndāvane
rādhā⋅kṛṣṇamaharniśaṁ prabhajatau jīvārthadau jau mudā
vande rūpa⋅sanātanau raghu⋅yugau śrī⋅jīva⋅gopālakau

saŋkhyāpūrvaka⋅nāma⋅gāna⋅natibhiḥ kālāvasānīkṛtau
nidrāhāra⋅vihārakādi⋅vijitau cātyantadīnau ca jau
rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa⋅guṇa⋅smṛtermadhurimānandena sammohitau
vande rūpa⋅sanātanau raghu⋅yugau śrī⋅jīva⋅gopālakau

rādhā⋅kuṇḍa⋅taṭe kalinda⋅tanayā⋅tīre ca vaṁśīvaṭe
premonmāda⋅vaśādaśeṣa⋅daśayā grastau pramattau sadā
gāyantau ca kadā harer⋅guṇa⋅varaṁ bhāvābhibhūtau mudā
vande rūpa⋅sanātanau raghu⋅yugau śrī⋅jīva⋅gopālakau

he rādhe! vraja⋅devike! ca lalite! he nandasūno! kutaḥ
śrī⋅govardhana⋅kalpa⋅pādapa⋅tale kālindī⋅vanye kutaḥ
ghoṣantāviti sarvato vraja⋅pure khedair⋅mahā⋅vihvalau
vande rūpa⋅sanātanau raghu⋅yugau śrī⋅jīva⋅gopālakau
en_translation:
Engaged in the congregational chanting of Kṛṣṇa's names, singing and dancing—they are oceans of the nectar of divine love. Dear to both the sober and the restless, they are doers of good, free from envy, and revered. Full of the mercy of Lord Caitanya and relieving the earth of its burden—I offer my obeisances to Rūpa, Sanātana, Raghunātha dāsa, Raghunātha Bhaṭṭa, Jīva, and Gopāla Bhaṭṭa.

Expert in deliberating on various scriptures and establishers of true religious principles, they are benefactors of humanity, respected in the three worlds, and abodes of refuge. Like intoxicated bees relishing the bliss of worshiping the lotus feet of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa—I offer my obeisances to Rūpa, Sanātana, Raghunātha dāsa, Raghunātha Bhaṭṭa, Jīva, and Gopāla Bhaṭṭa.

Endowed with abundant faith in the process of describing Lord Caitanya's qualities, they are the cutters of the miseries and sins of embodied beings through the nectar of the Lord's glories. Expert in increasing the ocean of bliss and delivering others from impersonal liberation—I offer my obeisances to Rūpa, Sanātana, Raghunātha dāsa, Raghunātha Bhaṭṭa, Jīva, and Gopāla Bhaṭṭa.

Having quickly given up all ranks of powerful leaders and always considering them insignificant, out of compassion they became the masters of the humble and poor. Taking shelter of simple loincloths and patched coverings, they are constantly immersed in the waves and surges of the nectar-ocean of the mood of the gopīs—I offer my obeisances to Rūpa, Sanātana, Raghunātha dāsa, Raghunātha Bhaṭṭa, Jīva, and Gopāla Bhaṭṭa.

In Vṛndāvana, which is filled with cooing cuckoos, swans, and cranes, crowded with peacocks, and adorned with trees whose roots are bound with jewels—they worship Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa day and night and joyfully bestow the ultimate benefit upon all souls. I offer my obeisances to Rūpa, Sanātana, Raghunātha dāsa, Raghunātha Bhaṭṭa, Jīva, and Gopāla Bhaṭṭa.

They utilized their time by chanting the Holy Names a fixed number of times and by offering obeisances. They conquered sleep, eating, and recreation, and were extremely humble. Enchanted by the sweet bliss of remembering the qualities of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa—I offer my obeisances to Rūpa, Sanātana, Raghunātha dāsa, Raghunātha Bhaṭṭa, Jīva, and Gopāla Bhaṭṭa.

On the bank of Rādhā-kuṇḍa, on the bank of the Yamunā, and at the Vaṁśīvaṭa tree, they were always overwhelmed by the madness of divine love, affected by various stages of ecstasy and always intoxicated. Sometimes they were joyfully overwhelmed by transcendental emotions while singing the excellent qualities of the Lord. I offer my obeisances to Rūpa, Sanātana, Raghunātha dāsa, Raghunātha Bhaṭṭa, Jīva, and Gopāla Bhaṭṭa.

"O Rādhā! O Goddess of Vraja! O Lalitā! O Son of Nanda! Where are You? Are You under the wish-fulfilling trees of Govardhana, or in the forest along the Yamunā?" Shouting in this way everywhere in Vraja and greatly agitated by the distress of separation—I offer my obeisances to Rūpa, Sanātana, Raghunātha dāsa, Raghunātha Bhaṭṭa, Jīva, and Gopāla Bhaṭṭa.
